title: Covering Retreat Operations
summary: Protecting withdrawing allied ships during retreat.
details: When allies retreat, healthy ships provide cover. Position between retreating ships and enemy. Draw enemy attention and fire away from retreating ships. Retreat in bounds: some ships withdraw while others cover, then swap. Last ships to leave are those best able to survive the withdrawal. Covering retreat is dangerous but essential - it saves more ships than holding position.
annotations: The rear guard accepts higher risk for squadron survival.
